title: Coleus praetermissus
taxonomy: Coleus praetermissus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a member of the genus Coleus, which is a group of plants native to tropical regions. The genus Coleus is further divided into several species, each with unique characteristics and growth habits. Coleus praetermissus is a relatively lesser-known species within the Coleus genus, but it is still valued for its ornamental value and hardy nature. The classification of Coleus praetermissus reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.

intro: Coleus praetermissus, also known as Coleus forskohlii, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa, characterized by its perennial growth habit and distinctive purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular support, due to its ability to stimulate the production of cyclic AMP (cAMP), a secondary messenger in the body. The bioactive constituents of Coleus praetermissus include forskolin, a diterpene compound, as well as other flavonoids and phenolic acids. Traditionally, Coleus praetermissus is used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and extracts, often combined with other herbs like turmeric and ginger, to support weight loss, improve cardiovascular health, and alleviate respiratory issues.
